title: 9. SHACL-style gates — clipboard before promote
description: Closed-world checks vs open-world OWL. Min evidence rules.
story:
OWL can describe meaning in an open world (missing fact ≠ false). SHACL is the clipboard at the dock: before promote, required fields and links must exist.

This app uses a Python lite validator with the same job: min symptoms, min failure modes, min INDICATES links, every link id must exist in the pack.
one_liner: Shapes fail-closed before promote; reasoner is optional offline, not diagnose-path.
why_it_matters: Bad ABox in production = wrong diagnosis at scale.
say_aloud: We shape-check ABox before materialize and promote.
Minimum evidence: at least one symptom, failure mode, and INDICATES link.
OWL open world is not the same as SHACL closed world.

beats.title: Min evidence rules
beats.narrative: If you ship empty products, ranking has nothing to stand on.
beats.say_after: Three mins; non-empty errors means block promote.
pro_tips: GOTCHA: sh:conforms is true ONLY if there are zero results — even a Warning-severity result fails conformance (W3C SHACL §3.6).
TRICK: non-empty results = block promote; read sh:sourceConstraintComponent to see exactly which rule fired.
TRICK: tier rules with sh:severity (Info/Warning/Violation); the default is Violation.
